title: TextFormat::Parser allows unbounded recursion and stack exhaustion

direct_impact.operation: recursive descent parsing exhausts thread stack
direct_impact.bytes: approximately 10000 nested messages in the confirmed reproduction

chain_potential.standalone: reliable denial of service against services that parse attacker-controlled textproto
chain_potential.with_other_bugs: limited direct chaining value because the confirmed outcome is stack exhaustion rather than controlled memory corruption

justification.AV: N - Exploitable through network-delivered textproto input when exposed by a service.
justification.AC: L - The attack only requires sending deeply nested but otherwise valid input; no race or special environment is needed.
justification.UI: N - The parser crashes during normal processing without user assistance.
justification.S: U - The impact is contained within the vulnerable service.
justification.C: N - No confidentiality impact was demonstrated.
justification.I: N - No integrity impact was demonstrated.
justification.A: H - The confirmed result is a reproducible SIGSEGV from stack exhaustion.

notes: Execution evidence shows valid inputs parse successfully until stack exhaustion at high nesting depth.
Scoring stays availability-only because the confirmed effect is crash, not code execution.
